what_it_does: Defines the Build canvas for ConceptV Studio: a React workflow where a user brief is planned into specialist subtasks, generated through agent-style helpers, composed into an output, and optionally refined, adopted, opened in Workshop, or saved as a template.
observations: Uses a four-stage workflow: brief, plan review, generation, and compose/adopt.
Seeds common starting briefs for brochures, palettes, icons, copy, slides, widgets, and wizards.
Plans work by prompting window.claude.complete for compact JSON subtasks and strategy text.
Runs non-composer subtasks in parallel, then runs one composer subtask after results are available.
Tracks subtask status values such as idle, active, done, and blocked.
Supports broad refinement plus targeted refinement for individual icons, colors, copy variants, whole sections, and the composed output.
Renders plan editing, stage navigation, generated-part previews, task status UI, composed output UI, and refinement controls in one file.
Uses global browser hooks such as window.claude, window.dsaToast, window.TypeLayerBadge, and window.CV_TYPES_PROMPT.
standouts: The file models generation as editable subtasks with exactly one composer step at the end.
The refinement path can rerun selected generators and then recompose the full output.
The UI exposes both workflow-level controls and per-asset refinement controls.
The implementation keeps prompt construction, async orchestration, state transitions, and rendering in the same module.
conventions: React function components with aliased hooks useState_b, useEffect_b, and useRef_b.
Inline style objects are used heavily for layout and visual treatment.
Agent outputs are expected as JSON and parsed with fallback extraction.
Subtask kinds are represented as string literals and styled through SUBTASK_META.
Callbacks from parent props handle adoption, navigation, template saving, and Workshop opening.
Status-driven rendering controls the visible workflow stage and action availability.
concerns: The plan prompt describes type-builder as available but the listed output kind union in the same prompt omits type-builder.
The visible JSX around the Output component appears to include an extra closing token: refining={refining}/>}/>.
Many responsibilities are file-local: prompt text, orchestration, parsing, UI rendering, and asset adoption logic.
Model responses are parsed defensively, but schema validation is limited to basic shape checks in the shown code.
The nested markActive and markDone helpers rely on current plan state inside asynchronous refinement flows.
notes: This file is the Build canvas orchestration surface: brief in, editable subtask plan, parallel generators, composer output, and iterative refinement.
questions: Should type-builder be accepted by generatePlan as a valid planned subtask kind?
Is the visible Output JSX closing sequence a transcription artifact or a file syntax issue?
What exact contracts do the generated widget, wizard, Workshop doc, and Type draft objects follow?
Should parsing and prompt schemas be shared with other canvases or remain local to Build.jsx?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose matches the implementation shown: it is a cross-canvas Vi planner with a layered task workflow that decomposes, generates, composes, and refines design-system outputs.
intention_signals: The file appears intended to make AI generation inspectable and controllable by turning a brief into editable specialist tasks, then preserving refinement and adoption actions inside the same canvas.
novelty: The distinct pattern is a design-system canvas that treats AI work as a visible task tree with targeted per-asset refinement and a final composing agent.
contribution: This file contributes a neutral orchestration layer for turning design briefs into structured, reviewable, generated design-system artifacts, including task planning, subtask execution, composed previews, refinement history, and adoption hooks.
summary_for_embedding: Build.jsx is a React canvas that lets ConceptV users submit a brief, review an AI-generated subtask plan, run specialist generators, compose the results, refine parts or the whole output, and adopt or save generated assets.
intention_for_embedding: It was built to make Vi-assisted design-system creation feel like an editable, staged workflow rather than a single opaque generation step.

symbols.does: Renders and orchestrates the full Build canvas workflow.
symbols.description: Build owns the brief, stage, plan, busy flags, errors, refinement input, and refinement history. It coordinates planning, generation, composition, targeted refinement, reset/back navigation, and passes generated outputs to adoption, template, navigation, and Workshop callbacks.

symbols.does: Prompts Vi to turn the current brief into a JSON plan of subtasks.
symbols.description: generatePlan clears prior plan state, sends a planning prompt to window.claude.complete, parses the returned JSON, adds local ids/status/result fields to each subtask, and advances the workflow to plan review. The prompt requires one composer subtask at the end and describes the available specialist agents.

symbols.does: Updates the brief text for one planned subtask.
symbols.description: This helper maps over the current plan subtasks and replaces the brief for the subtask matching the provided id. It supports manual editing during the plan review stage.

symbols.does: Updates the display label for one planned subtask.
symbols.description: This helper maps over the current plan subtasks and replaces the label for the matching id. It lets the plan editor keep user edits in local React state.

symbols.does: Removes a subtask from the current plan.
symbols.description: removeSubtask filters the plan subtasks by id. The plan editor exposes this for removable non-composer subtasks while the composer is kept separately.

symbols.does: Runs all non-composer subtasks and then starts composition.
symbols.description: runGeneration moves to the generation stage, marks non-composer subtasks active, runs them with Promise.all through runSubtask, records done or blocked status per subtask, and calls runCompose with the collected results. It keeps the composer idle until generation results exist.

symbols.does: Runs the composer subtask using generated results.
symbols.description: runCompose moves the workflow to stage four, marks the composer active, calls runComposer with the composer brief, main brief, and part results, and stores either the composed result or a blocked error result. It clears the composing flag in a finally block.

symbols.does: Re-runs composition from the current non-composer results.
symbols.description: recompose collects all existing non-composer subtask results from the current plan and passes them to runCompose. It is a local recomposition helper for rebuilding the final output without regenerating parts.

symbols.does: Returns the Build workflow to its initial editable-brief state.
symbols.description: reset sets the stage back to one, clears the current plan and error, and empties refinement state. It preserves the current brief value rather than restoring a seed.

symbols.does: Moves back to the brief stage when the workflow is idle.
symbols.description: backToBrief prevents navigation while planning, generating, composing, or refining is active. When allowed, it sets the stage to one.

symbols.does: Moves back to the plan review stage when the workflow is idle.
symbols.description: backToPlan blocks navigation during busy states and otherwise sets the stage to two. It is used from later stages to edit the plan again.

symbols.does: Applies a targeted refinement to a specific generated part or section.
symbols.description: refinePart handles scopes for one icon, one color, one copy variant, all icons, all colors, all copy, or the composed output. It prompts the model or reruns the relevant generator, preserves prior results on some failures, reruns the composer with current parts, logs the refinement, and shows toast feedback.

symbols.does: Marks a subtask active inside refinePart.
symbols.description: markActive is a local state helper nested in refinePart. It updates the plan by setting the matching subtask status to active before a refinement or recomposition run.

symbols.does: Marks a subtask done with a supplied result inside refinePart.
symbols.description: markDone is a local state helper nested in refinePart. It updates the matching subtask status to done and stores the replacement result after targeted refinement succeeds or after restoring a previous result.

symbols.does: Applies a freeform refinement request across the build.
symbols.description: refine asks Vi which generator subtasks need to rerun for the user's change, updates those subtask briefs and statuses, reruns only the selected generators, then recomposes using updated and retained parts. It records the refinement summary and changed kinds in refinement history.

symbols.does: Renders the four-step workflow navigation strip.
symbols.description: Stepper displays Brief, Plan, Generate, and Compose with active, done, and running visual states. Completed prior steps can be clicked through the supplied onJumpTo callback when the parent allows it.

symbols.does: Renders editable cards for planned subtasks.
symbols.description: PlanEditor separates non-composer subtasks from the composer, lays non-composers out in a grid, and renders the composer as a downstream wide card. It passes label, brief, and remove handlers to PlanEditorCard.

symbols.does: Renders one editable planned subtask card.
symbols.description: PlanEditorCard shows the subtask kind metadata, an editable label input, an editable brief textarea, and an optional remove button. It uses SUBTASK_META for color and display label.

symbols.does: Shows generated icons, colors, copy, and Type drafts as they become available.
symbols.description: PartsPreview reads completed generator results from the plan and renders compact previews for icon SVG bodies, color swatches, copy variants, and type draft cards. It returns null when no previewable parts exist.

symbols.does: Renders one explanatory card for the Build workflow.
symbols.description: BuildHowCard displays a layer badge, title, and description for the brief, plan, generate, and compose explanation section. It is used in stage one to explain the canvas flow.

symbols.does: Renders the list or tree of build subtasks.
symbols.description: TaskList is the task-status display for the generation stage. From its usage, it receives the plan subtasks and presents their current progress through TaskNode-style rows or nodes.

symbols.does: Renders one task node in the generation status view.
symbols.description: TaskNode represents a single subtask with status and metadata. Its wide prop indicates it can adapt layout for the composer or another emphasized task node.

symbols.does: Renders the composed output and adoption controls.
symbols.description: Output receives the final plan, original brief, adoption callbacks, template callback, navigation callbacks, Workshop opening callback, and part-refinement callback. It is the stage-four surface for previewing generated work, adopting icons or colors, saving the run, and triggering targeted refinements.

symbols.does: Saves the current build run as a reusable template.
symbols.description: saveTemplate is an async helper associated with Output. Its role follows from the onSaveAsTemplate callback and stage text that offers saving the whole run as a reusable template.

symbols.does: Renders a visual preview from the composer result and generated parts.
symbols.description: ComposedPreview receives a preview object plus icons, colors, and copy. It is the file-local component for presenting the assembled result after the composer has combined generated assets.

symbols.does: Renders a generated Workshop document result.
symbols.description: WorkshopDocResult receives a document-producing subtask, an open callback, and the original brief. It provides the UI path for opening generated deck, brochure, widget, or wizard-style outputs in Workshop-facing flows.

symbols.does: Opens a generated Workshop document from WorkshopDocResult.
symbols.description: open is a local handler associated with WorkshopDocResult. It bridges the rendered generated document result to the provided onOpen callback.

symbols.does: Dispatches a planned subtask to the matching generator helper.
symbols.description: runSubtask is the central execution switch for subtask kinds. It is called by runGeneration and refine to route icons, colors, copy, widget, wizard, Workshop document, Type, and composer-related work to their specific helpers.

symbols.does: Generates a Type Registry draft for a reusable pattern.
symbols.description: runTypeBuilder is the helper for type-builder subtasks. The surrounding UI expects type-builder results with kind type-draft and a draft object containing fields such as name, layer, and description for review and registration.

symbols.does: Generates a ConceptV widget output.
symbols.description: runWidget is the helper for widget-builder subtasks. The planning prompt assigns this kind to dashboard tiles, hub panels, partner embeds, and public-facing live data displays.

symbols.does: Generates a multi-step flow output.
symbols.description: runWizard is the helper for wizard-builder subtasks. The planning prompt associates it with Property Wizard, onboarding, signup, and generic form flows.

symbols.does: Generates a Workshop document for a requested document type.
symbols.description: runWorkshopDoc takes a type, sub-brief, and main brief. It supports document-style subtasks such as decks and brochures described by the planner.

symbols.does: Generates SVG icon results for an icons subtask.
symbols.description: runIcons is used by icons-generator subtasks and by section-level icon refinement. The visible UI expects it to return an object with an icons array containing names and SVG body markup for 24 by 24 line icons.

symbols.does: Generates named hex colors for a colors subtask.
symbols.description: runColors is used by colors-generator subtasks and section-level color refinement. The visible UI expects it to return a colors array with name, hex, and role fields.

symbols.does: Generates short copy variants for a copy subtask.
symbols.description: runCopy is used by copy-writer subtasks and section-level copy refinement. The visible UI expects it to return a variants array of strings.

symbols.does: Generates the final composed output from subtask results.
symbols.description: runComposer receives the composer subtask, the main brief, and generated part results. It is called after generation and after refinements to assemble or refresh the preview stored on the composer subtask.

symbols.does: Renders a labeled refinement control for a target scope.
symbols.description: RefineChip provides a UI affordance for sending a scoped refinement request through onRefine. It receives the refinement scope, disabled state, visible label, and placeholder text for the request.

symbols.does: Submits a refinement action from a local refinement control.
symbols.description: This go function is a local handler associated with RefineChip. It likely collects the chip input and calls the provided refinement callback for that chip's scope.

symbols.does: Renders a compact refinement control for a target scope.
symbols.description: RefineDot is a smaller targeted refinement affordance. It receives a scope, refinement callback, disabled state, and placeholder text for local refinement input.

symbols.does: Submits a refinement action from the RefineDot control.
symbols.description: This second go function is a local handler associated with RefineDot. The line-qualified code id distinguishes it from the go handler used by RefineChip.

symbols.does: Parses JSON from model output with a loose fallback.
symbols.description: parseJsonLoose is used where model replies may include extra text around JSON. It attempts to recover a JSON object from the reply so refinement handlers can consume structured results.

symbols.does: Parses model output as JSON for generator helpers.
symbols.description: parseJson is the stricter JSON parsing helper used by generation functions. It centralizes conversion from window.claude.complete text replies into JavaScript objects expected by the Build workflow.
